This means that in your case, it cannot run.

SidebarMenu.updateBoardConfirmDescription: This board's contents will be replaced by the file's, keeping its place in your drawer. The drawer copy updates only when you Save. This can't be undone.
SidebarMenu.updateNoteConfirmDescription: This note's contents will be replaced by the file's, keeping its place in your drawer and any links pointing at it. The drawer copy updates only when you Save. This can't be undone.

SidebarMenu.resetConfirmDescription: This action is destructive. Your active character will be deleted and replaced with a blank character. If you don't reload the app, you can still recover your old character with the undo functionality.
SidebarMenu.clearBoardConfirmTitle: Are you sure you want to clear the board?
SidebarMenu.clearBoardConfirmDescription: This removes every item from the board. The board's name, position, and grid are kept, and you can bring the items back with the undo functionality (as long as you don't reload the app).

seeds.title: Generate from seeds (optional)
seeds.primaryInfo: Your main action color, for buttons and highlights.
seeds.backgroundInfo: The hue your surfaces are tinted with.
seeds.accentInfo: A second highlight color, distinct from the primary.
seeds.secondaryInfo: A quieter color for secondary buttons and surfaces.
seeds.saturationMinimalDesc: Near-grey surfaces; the calmest, most neutral look.
seeds.saturationBalancedDesc: A moderate tint; colorful but easy on the eyes.
seeds.saturationVividDesc: Boldly tinted surfaces and a strong accent.
seeds.contrastNormalDesc: The standard spread, like the built-in presets.
seeds.contrastContrastedDesc: A wider spread and crisper borders for more punch.
seeds.confirmDescription: This replaces all of the theme's colors with ones derived from the seeds. Your roundness is kept, and you can keep editing afterwards.

tokenPurpose.background: The app's main canvas color. The backdrop behind everything.
tokenPurpose.foreground: Default text and icons on the app canvas.
tokenPurpose.card: Raised panels that sit on the canvas, like dialogs, cards and sheets.
tokenPurpose.card-foreground: Text on raised panels and cards.
tokenPurpose.popover: Floating surfaces that pop over the page, like menus, dropdowns and tooltips.
tokenPurpose.popover-foreground: Text inside floating menus and popovers.
tokenPurpose.primary: Your main action color, used for primary buttons and key highlights.
tokenPurpose.primary-foreground: Text and icons on the primary action color.
tokenPurpose.ring: The focus outline around whatever control you've tabbed onto.
tokenPurpose.secondary: Secondary surfaces and buttons, the quieter alternative to the primary action.
tokenPurpose.secondary-foreground: Text on secondary surfaces and buttons.
tokenPurpose.muted: Subtle, inactive surfaces, like disabled fields, skeletons and gentle backdrops.
tokenPurpose.muted-foreground: Secondary, de-emphasized text, like captions, hints and placeholders.
tokenPurpose.accent: The hover and highlight surface. What a row or button turns when you point at it.
tokenPurpose.accent-foreground: Text on a hovered or highlighted surface.
tokenPurpose.destructive: Dangerous, irreversible actions, like delete, remove and reset.
tokenPurpose.destructive-foreground: Text and icons on the destructive color.
tokenPurpose.border: Dividers and outlines between sections and around panels.
tokenPurpose.input: The outline around text fields and inputs.

paper.title: Paper
paper.intro: The look of game-agnostic paper elements like drawer trackers. The same in light and dark.

tokenPurpose.paper-background: The parchment behind a paper element, like a drawer tracker's body.
tokenPurpose.paper-foreground: The ink: default text on the paper.
tokenPurpose.paper-primary: The header bar across the top of a paper element.
tokenPurpose.paper-primary-foreground: Text and icons on the header bar.
tokenPurpose.paper-secondary: Quieter chips and surfaces on the paper, like tags.
tokenPurpose.paper-border: The outline around a paper element and its dividers.
tokenPurpose.paper-accent: The standout accent on paper, like a highlighted edge.
tokenPurpose.paper-destructive: Dangerous bits on paper, like a burn or remove.

InfoDialog.title: About
InfoDialog.description: Information, credits, and licensing for this application.

content.about:
#### What is Campaigns of the Mist?

Campaigns of the Mist is an **unofficial**, fan-made character creator and manager for the TTRPGs from [Amit Moshe and Son of Oak Game Studio LLC](https://cityofmist.co). It's built from the ground up with a modern and responsive design, and all your data is saved directly in your browser for complete privacy and control. This tool is in no way endorsed by Amit Moshe or Son of Oak Game Studio LLC.

---

#### What can this tool do?

This tool is meant to help you create and update your character sheet quickly and easily. It allows you to edit your Theme Cards, Statuses, and Name, and save everything automatically. Beyond the basics, it's packed with powerful features to make managing your character a breeze:

* A versatile **Drawer** that acts like a complete file system for your character components, with full drag-and-drop organization and a compact view for managing many items.

* Intuitive **drag-and-drop** for almost everything: reorder your cards and trackers, move items between folders, or even load an entire character by dragging it from the drawer.

* A fully **dynamic character sheet** with flexible cards. You can even switch to a **side-by-side view** if you prefer to see the front and back of cards at the same time.

* A powerful **Undo & Redo system** (`Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Y`) that will allow you to fix your mistakes whether they happened in the Drawer or on your Character Sheet.

* A **Command Palette** (`Ctrl+K` or `Cmd+K`) that lets you find and use any feature in the app without touching your mouse.

* **Game-related personalization** with multiple color themes assorted to Mist TTRPGs and a light/dark mode toggle to make the app feel like your own.

---

#### Can this tool be used as a VTT?

As it is right now, this tool is only a character manager. It doesn't support battlemaps, handouts, dice rollers or further automatization. You are free to fork this project and add those features, as long as the license terms are respected.

---

#### Can I report a problem with the app?

You can send me a message on [the official Tools of the Mist discord](https://discord.gg/c7GBPyTcWW) should you encounter a problem with the app, and I will see what I can do about it. Keep in mind however that this app is distributed AS-IS, with no guarantees whatsoever.
content.license:
#### What license is this code under?

This app is under the [GNU Affero General Public License v3.0](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl-3.0.html).

This means you are free to use, copy, modify, and redistribute the code, including for commercial purposes. However, if you distribute or run a modified version (including hosting it for others over a network), you must make your full source code available under this same license.
content.credits:
#### Credits & Attributions

Thank you to [Son of Oak Game Studio LLC](https://cityofmist.co) for creating City of Mist, Metro:OTHERSCAPE and Legend in the Mist. The City of Mist Logos and Mythos icons, as well as the :Otherscape Noise, Self and Mythos icons are the intellectual property of Son of Oak Game Studios. I do not claim any ownership on these icons, and will remove them from the app should it be requested.
content.localization:
#### Localization

Thank you to everyone who helped make Campaigns of the Mist available in languages other than English!

LegacyDataDialog.confirmationLabel: If you wish to dismiss your data, write the following confirmation text in the field below:

WelcomeDialog.title: Welcome to Campaigns of the Mist!
WelcomeDialog.description: This is an unofficial, fan-made character manager for TTRPGs by Son of Oak Game Studios. All your data is saved in your browser, ensuring complete privacy.

CommandPalette.placeholder_3: Summon the Will of the Gods...
CommandPalette.placeholder_9: You don't need to hear this, you're a highly trained professional...
CommandPalette.placeholder_11: Let me guess, somebody stole your sweetroll...
CommandPalette.placeholder_15: I was crazy once, they put me in a room, a rubber room...
CommandPalette.placeholder_16: Wake up. This is a dream...
CommandPalette.placeholder_17: You again? Do you have no mouse? Oh well...
CommandPalette.placeholder_18: Something's wrong, I can feel it...
CommandPalette.placeholder_19: The Phantom Thieves are going to steal your heart...
CommandPalette.placeholder_20: I hope you paid Tom Nook back...
CommandPalette.placeholder_21: The Sun is a deadly laser...
CommandPalette.placeholder_24: May Zeus himself strike me down if I'm ly-...
CommandPalette.placeholder_25: This has got to be the worst copper I've ever seen...

Tutorial.anchorMissing: This part of the app has changed, so the tutorial is ending here.

navigation.welcome_body: Here is a short tour of the app, and it should take about two minutes. You can leave at any time by pressing the X or the Esc key, and you can start it again whenever you like from your Settings.
navigation.sidebar_body: This is the Sidebar, and it stays on the left at all times. From here you can open your Drawer, undo and redo your actions, save, export or import a character, and reach your Settings.
navigation.homeBase_body: This screen is your home base, the one you land on when no workspace is open. From here you can pick a game to start a new character, or open a board to begin building your world.
navigation.collapse_body: This button collapses the Sidebar down to a narrow strip of icons. It gives you more room for your workspace, and you can expand it again the same way.
navigation.undoRedo_body: Nearly everything you do can be undone. These buttons step your actions backward and forward, and you can also use Ctrl+Z and Ctrl+Y on your keyboard.
navigation.tabs_body: These are your Tabs. Each one is a separate workspace, and it can hold a character, a board, or a set of notes. You can keep several open at once and switch between them here, so a character sheet, a board and your notes are always a click apart.
navigation.newTab_body: The plus button opens a new workspace, and you can choose any type you like: a character, a board, or a set of notes. Each one opens in its own tab alongside the others.
navigation.playArea_body: The active workspace fills the center of the screen. Right now it holds a sample character for you to explore, and this is where your characters, boards and notes will appear as you work.
navigation.openDrawer_body: Let's take a look at the Drawer. Press the Drawer button here to open it.
navigation.drawerTour_body: This is your Drawer, where everything you save is stored. You can keep characters, cards and trackers here, organize them into folders, and drag any of them onto a sheet or a board. The Drawer has its own lesson if you want to go deeper.
navigation.openNavigator_body: Next is the Navigator. Press the Navigator button here to open it.
navigation.navigatorTour_body: The Navigator maps the links between your boards and notes. As your campaign grows, you can use it to hop from one board or note to another across your whole project, all from a single tree.
navigation.openDice_body: Now for the Dice Tray. Press the Dice Tray button here to slide it open.
navigation.diceTour_body: This is the Dice Tray. You can build a pool of dice and roll it here, and it stays with you as you move between tabs, so your dice are always within reach.
navigation.openSettings_body: Let's open your Settings. Press the Settings button here to open the hub.
navigation.settingsGeneral_body: This is the General section. Here you can set your language and adjust how the app behaves from day to day, like the way your cards are shown and whether your trackers stay locked while you play.
navigation.settingsAppearance_body: The Appearance section is where you set the look of the app. You can choose a color palette, switch between light and dark mode, and manage your own custom themes.
navigation.settingsData_body: The Data and storage section keeps your saved work in view. You can see how much space you are using, reclaim it, and reset the app if you ever need a clean start.
navigation.settingsLearn_body: This is the Learn section of your Settings. Every lesson, including this one, lives here, and you can replay any of them whenever you like.
navigation.settingsWhatsNew_body: The What's new section gathers the release notes for the app. Whenever an update lands, you can come here to read what has changed and what has been added.
navigation.settingsAbout_body: The About section holds the details about the app, including its license and its credits. If you ever need help or want to report a problem, you will find where to reach me here.
navigation.commandPalette_body: The command palette lets you run any action by name, from anywhere in the app. It is open here so you can see it, and you can bring it up yourself at any time by pressing Ctrl and K together. Type the name of a workspace to jump to it, or the name of an action to run it.
navigation.wrap_body: That is the shape of the app. Take your time to explore, and remember that you can replay any lesson from the Learn section in your Settings.

sheet.overview_body: This is your Character Sheet. Your trackers sit across the top and your cards fill the space below, and together they hold everything about your character.
sheet.name_body: The title at the top is your character's name. You can click it to edit at any time, and your changes are saved automatically.
sheet.modes_body: Your Character Sheet has two modes, Play and Edit. Play mode is for using your character during a session, while Edit mode is where you build it and make changes, like adding cards and trackers. You can switch between them with this toggle.
sheet.enterEdit_body: Let's start building. Flip this toggle into Edit mode so the tools for adding and changing things appear.
sheet.trackers_body: This section is where your trackers live. They hold your character's fluid state during play, like their statuses and their story tags, and you can find them all listed here.
sheet.addStatus_body: Statuses are temporary conditions on your character, like Wounded or Empowered, each with a tier. Add one now using this button to see how it works.
sheet.addStoryTag_body: Story tags are narrative details you can invoke during play, like a helpful ally or a locked door. Add one now using this button.
sheet.cards_body: Cards are the heart of your sheet. They hold your theme cards, your hero card, your journals and your portraits, and you can drag any of them to reorder your sheet.
sheet.addMenu_body: Everything you can add to your sheet lives behind this plus button. From here you can create a Card, a Portrait, a Challenge, or a Journal.
sheet.openCardDialog_body: This is where you add new cards to your sheet. Click it, then choose Card from the menu that opens to start a new one.
sheet.createCard_body: Now you can pick the type of card you want to create. Choose one and it will be added to your sheet, ready for you to fill in.
sheet.addJournal_body: A journal is a free-form space for notes that lives right on your sheet. Open the same menu, choose Journal, and add one now.
sheet.addPortrait_body: A portrait gives your character a face on the sheet. Open the menu once more, choose Portrait, and add one to see it appear.
sheet.playMode_body: Now that your sheet is built, flip the toggle back to Play mode to use everything you just made.
sheet.save_body: This button saves your character into your Drawer, keeping a copy in your library that you can come back to later.
sheet.exportImport_body: You can export your character to a file to back it up or share it with someone, and the file will end in .cotm. Import does the reverse, loading a character file back into the app.
sheet.createCharacter_body: When you want a fresh character, you start from this home menu, or from the plus button in the tab bar. Pick a game and you are ready to build a new one from scratch.
sheet.wrap_body: You have built a sheet from start to finish. You can replay this lesson any time from the Learn section in your Settings.

mobileSheet.overview_body: This is your character sheet. It holds your trackers and your cards, and everything about your character lives here.
mobileSheet.name_body: The title up top is your character's name. Tap it to rename, and your changes are saved as you type.
mobileSheet.tabs_body: Your sheet has two halves. Tap between the Trackers tab and the Cards tab to move from one to the other.
mobileSheet.trackers_body: This is where your trackers live: statuses, story tags, and story themes. They hold your character's state during play.
mobileSheet.toolbelt_body: Your tools live here. This is where you find Edit mode, the dice, and the buttons to add to your sheet.
mobileSheet.enterEdit_body: Let's start building. Tap Edit to unlock the tools for adding and changing things.
mobileSheet.addStatus_body: Statuses are temporary conditions like Wounded or Empowered, each with a tier. Tap here to add one.
mobileSheet.addMore_body: Story tags and story themes add the same way, with their own buttons right here.
mobileSheet.trackerSelect_body: Press and hold a tracker to select it. The toolbelt then acts on whatever you have selected.
mobileSheet.cards_body: Now the other half. Tap the Cards tab to switch over to your cards.
mobileSheet.cardSwipe_body: Swipe left or right across a card to move through them one at a time.
mobileSheet.cardNav_body: The bar below your cards helps you get around. Use the arrows to step through, tap a dot to jump, and tap a card to flip it.
mobileSheet.openReorder_body: Rearranging your cards has its own mode, with the whole deck as a list. Tap Reorder to open it.
mobileSheet.reorderDrag_body: Press and hold a card's grip handle first, then drag it up or down the list. A drag on its own will not pick the card up. Tapping a card jumps straight to it, and the check button saves your order when it looks right.
mobileSheet.wrap_body: That is your character sheet, top to bottom. You can replay this lesson any time from the Learn section in your Settings.

mobileNav.welcome_body: A quick look at where everything lives: the three places you work, your tools, and your settings. Nothing you have is changed along the way.
mobileNav.navControl_body: The bar along the bottom is how you move around. It holds the three places you work, plus your toolbelt, and it stays with you wherever you go.
mobileNav.navControl_body_fab: This floating button is how you move around. Tapping it opens a ring holding the three places you work, plus your settings, and it stays with you wherever you go.
mobileNav.openRing_body: Give it a tap. Your destinations fan out around it.
mobileNav.sheetDest_body: The Sheet is your character: trackers on one half, cards on the other. With no character loaded there is nothing to open, so it greys out until you make or load one.
mobileNav.drawerDest_body: The Drawer is your library. Every character, card and tracker you save goes in here, in folders you arrange yourself. Tap it to have a look.
mobileNav.drawerLanding_body: Your library, in full. Search it, sort it into folders, and pull pieces onto your sheet from here. The Drawer lesson walks through all of that properly.
mobileNav.drawerLanding_body_fab: Your library, in full. Notice the ring closed itself on the way in: picking a destination puts it away, so it is never sitting on top of the page you asked for. The Drawer lesson walks through the rest of this properly.
mobileNav.reopenRing_body: The button never leaves. Tap it again to open the ring and pick somewhere new.
mobileNav.menuDest_body: The Menu is home. It is where a character begins, and where you land whenever none is loaded. Tap it to head back.
mobileNav.menuContent_body: Back home. Pick your game system here, then create a character or import one from a file. Either one replaces the character you have loaded, so you are always warned first, with the chance to save it to the Drawer.
mobileNav.toolbeltLocator_body: The wrench holds your workspace tools: Edit mode, the dice, and everything you can add. It lights up on your sheet, where those tools have something to work on. The Character Sheet lesson covers using them.
mobileNav.toolbeltLocator_body_fab: Your sheet has a second floating button, the wrench, sitting just above the navigation one. It holds your workspace tools: Edit mode, the dice, and everything you can add. The Character Sheet lesson covers using them.
mobileNav.settingsEntry_body: Settings sits right at the top of the Menu. Tap it to open the hub.
mobileNav.settingsEntry_body_fab: Settings has its own place in the ring, so it is one tap away from anywhere. Tap it to open the hub.
mobileNav.settingsContent_body: And this is the hub, holding everything that is not the three places you work. General, Appearance and Data for setting things up, then What's new and About. Learn is where these lessons live, so you can replay any of them whenever you like.
mobileNav.wrap_body: Sheet, Drawer and Menu are the three places you work, your tools ride along with them, and Settings holds the rest. You can replay this lesson any time from the Learn section in your Settings.

mobileDrawer.welcome_body: The Drawer is where everything you save lives: characters, cards, trackers, boards and notes, filed into folders you arrange yourself. This lesson runs on a small sample library, so nothing you have saved is touched along the way.
mobileDrawer.content_body: Folders sit at the top, and everything saved in the folder you are standing in comes below them. Each saved item shows you what it is, which game it belongs to, and when you last worked on it.
mobileDrawer.search_body: Search by name from here, and it looks through your whole library rather than just the folder you are in. The sliders button beside the field opens Filters, which narrows a search down by kind, by game, or by when something was made or last changed.
mobileDrawer.viewToggle_body: This button switches your saved items between compact rows and rich previews. Compact fits far more on screen at once; rich shows you a proper look at each one, so you can tell two similar things apart before you open either.
mobileDrawer.folders_body: Tap a folder to go inside it, and everything filed in there takes over the list. The trail along the bottom always shows where you are, and tapping any step of it walks you back out.
mobileDrawer.itemMenu_body: Every saved item carries this button, and everything you can do with that item is behind it: add it to your sheet, load it, rename it, move it to another folder, export it to a file, or delete it for good.
mobileDrawer.reorder_body: Press and hold an item anywhere on its row, keep holding until it lifts, then drag it up or down. There is no separate handle here, the row itself is the grip, which is why the hold has to be a deliberate one: a quick tap does nothing, and a fast swipe just scrolls the list. Have a go, then carry on. Folders move the same way.
mobileDrawer.toolbar_body: Along the bottom: a new folder, importing a saved file back into your library, and the view switch. The two arrows on the other side undo and redo whatever you change in here, so a move, a rename or a delete is never final.
mobileDrawer.wrap_body: Save your work into it, sort it into folders that suit you, and pull any of it back out onto your sheet whenever you need it. You can replay this lesson any time from the Learn section in your Settings.

mobileThemes.welcome_body: The whole look of the app is a theme, a set of colors you can change or build yourself. This lesson opens your Appearance settings, tries a ready-made theme, and points you to where you can make your own. Whatever you pick here is put back the way you found it when you leave.
mobileThemes.mode_body: This sets the overall mode. Light and Dark pin it one way, and System follows whatever your phone is set to. It is the coarse choice you make before the colors themselves.
mobileThemes.themeList_body: Below the mode sit your themes: the presets that ship with the app, and any you have made yourself, further down. Tapping one applies it at once, and the whole app takes on its colors.
mobileThemes.applyPreset_body: Tap a preset to put it on, and watch the colors change around you as the app re-skins. Pick whichever one you like, and the lesson moves on.
mobileThemes.makeYourOwn_body: This button opens the theme editor on a fresh theme. Inside, you set each color by hand for both light and dark, with a live preview as you work, and a seed generator can grow a whole palette from a single color for a fast start. Presets cannot be edited in place, so a saved theme's menu hands you an editable copy to build from.
mobileThemes.importExport_body: A theme you make can travel. This button imports one from a file, and each of your own themes can be exported the same way from its menu. It is how you hand a palette to another player or carry one between your devices.
mobileThemes.wrap_body: You have set the mode, tried a theme, and seen where to build your own. The theme is put back the way you found it, so nothing here changed your setup. You can replay this lesson any time from the Learn section in your Settings.

board.welcome_body: The Board is a freeform canvas for your table. You can lay out a scene, a map of clues, or a cast of characters, arranged however you think about them, so your whole game has a place to live.
board.canvas_body: This is the canvas. It stretches as far as you need in every direction, and everything you place lives at its own spot in that space, so you can spread your work out and come back to it later.
board.handleItems_body: Drag any element to move it, and click one to select it. To select several at once, drag a box across the empty background. Double-click an element to open or edit it. Give it a try, then continue.
board.panZoom_body: To move around the canvas, drag with the right mouse button, or hold Alt and drag. Scroll to zoom in and out. Try it now to get a feel for it, then continue when you are ready.
board.toolbar_body: The bar along the bottom holds everything you need. From here you can switch between the two work modes, add or draw whatever you like, open the Layers panel, and use the view controls that recenter and zoom the canvas.
board.modes_body: The Board has two modes. Elements mode is where you place things like cards and notes, while Drawing mode lets you sketch and mark up the canvas directly. You can switch between them with this toggle.
board.addMenu_body: In Elements mode, everything you can place lives behind this Add button. From here you can drop notes, portals, game cards, trackers, and more onto the board.
board.dropElement_body: Open the Add menu and drop something onto the board. A Post-it or a Pin is the quickest way to start, and it will land in the middle of your view.
board.radialMenu_body: There is a faster way to add things: right-click anywhere on the canvas to open a radial menu, and whatever you pick drops right where you clicked. Give it a try, then continue.
board.drawTools_body: In Drawing mode you first pick a gesture, either freehand, straight lines, shapes, or the eraser, and then set the brush, its size, and its ink. The New layer button starts your next stroke on a layer of its own.
board.drawStroke_body: Now draw something on the canvas by pressing and dragging across it. Anything you like will do.
board.shapes_body: The shape tools drag out clean circles, squares, and polygons, which are useful for marking zones and regions on your map. You can pick one and drag out a shape if you like, then continue.
board.backToElements_body: Back in Elements mode, you can select, move, and arrange everything you have placed on the board.
board.layersToggle_body: Let's look at the Layers panel. Press the Layers button here to open it.
board.layersPanel_body: This lists every element on the board, stacked from the top of the list to the front of the canvas. You can hover a row to find an element, click to select it, and drag to restack it, and you can group related things together into zones. Drawing layers can be merged from here too.
board.connections_body: Elements can be linked to one another. These two cards are joined by a connection line, which is a good way to show how things relate. To make your own, you select an element and drag its connect handle onto another, and you can select a line to recolor it, change its arrow, or remove it.
board.dice_body: The Dice Tray rides along here too, so you can build a pool and roll it without ever leaving the board.
board.wrap_body: That is the Board. You can replay this lesson any time from the Learn section in your Settings.

portals.welcome_body: A portal is a link from one workspace to another, so a board can point to a note and a note can point to another board. This lesson shows how to make one and how to follow it, then opens the Navigator, the map that reads your whole web of links.
portals.whatIsPortal_body: This tile is a portal. It stands in for another workspace and carries you there when you dive through it. To dive, you double-click the tile, and you land in whatever it points to.
portals.makeALink_body: You make a portal from the Add menu. Choose Portal, then pick the board, note, or character you want it to point to, and the tile drops onto the board ready to dive through. The picker lists your own workspaces, so leave it be for now.
portals.openNavigator_body: One portal is easy to follow, but a web of them is easier to read as a map. That map is the Navigator, and this button on the side opens it.
portals.readTheTree_body: The Navigator draws your links as a tree. The ringed row at the top is where you are now, and the rows beneath it are the places its portals lead. Each of those can branch again, so the whole path is laid out in one view.
portals.crawl_body: A row with a caret leads somewhere further. Click the caret to open it and see where that link goes, without leaving where you are.
portals.jump_body: Reading the tree is one thing, and going there is another. Double-click a row to dive through the link and open that workspace for real.
portals.mapRecenters_body: You landed in the linked workspace, and the tree redrew around it. The ringed row is now where you are, and its own links branch below, so the Navigator always maps outward from wherever you stand.
portals.backTrail_body: The bar that appeared above tracks your dive, one crumb per stop. The Back arrow steps you out the way you came, and any crumb takes you straight to that stop, so a long dive never strands you.
portals.wrap_body: You have made a link, read the map, and followed a dive out and back. You can replay this lesson any time from the Learn section in your Settings.

notes.welcome_body: A note is a page for your words. You can keep session prep, a handout to read aloud, or the lore behind a place, and set it beside a character sheet or a board so the writing lives with the rest of your game.
notes.surface_body: This is a note, opened on its own page. It starts in Live mode, the one where you both read and write the same document, so what you see here is exactly how the note reads.
notes.livePreview_body: In Live mode you type into the finished page. The heading, the bold words, the list, and the quote all show the way they will read, and you edit them right where they sit. Look it over, then continue.
notes.typeMarkdown_body: Find the blank line beneath Your turn and write a few words. Start a line with # for a heading, with - for a list, or wrap a word in ** for bold. The page catches up a moment after you type, and the tour moves on once it does.
notes.mentions_body: The colored pills are mentions, a status or a tag written inside braces. In a note they are a visual reference, a tidy way to note the statuses and tags in play. The same braces are live elsewhere: write one in a journal or a challenge on a character sheet, or on a board, and clicking the pill there creates that tag or status for real.
notes.linksOut_body: A note can link to a heading inside itself, to another note, board, or character, or out to the web. This button inserts one. Links and the Navigator that maps them have a tutorial of their own, so start there when you want the full tour.
notes.toolbar_body: Every bit of formatting has a button here: headings, lists, quotes, tables, images, and links. You can type the markdown yourself or click these instead, whichever feels faster in the moment.
notes.images_body: Notes hold images too. Add one with this button, paste it from your clipboard, or drag a file onto the page. Once it lands you can set its size, choose its aspect ratio, and pick which side of the page it aligns to.
notes.cover_body: A note can also wear a cover, a banner image across the top of the page. Add one from this button, then set how wide it runs and its aspect ratio to frame the page the way you like.
notes.modes_body: This toggle switches how you view the note. Live is where you write, Reading shows the clean handout with nothing to edit, and Source shows the raw markdown behind it. Live is home, and the other two are there when you want them.
notes.wrap_body: You have typed in a note, met mentions and links, and seen the three modes. To make your own, add a note from the tab menu or the command palette, and find it later in the Drawer with the rest of your files. You can replay this lesson any time from the Learn section in your Settings.

drawer.welcome_body: The Drawer is your library. Every character, board, note, and card you save is kept here, ready to open again or drop into whatever you are working on. This lesson opens it, sorts it into folders, searches it, and expands it to the full width when you want the space.
drawer.open_body: The Drawer rests closed at the edge of the screen until you call for it. This button on the side opens it. Click it now to slide the Drawer out.
drawer.folders_body: Folders sort your library the way shelves sort a bookcase. Open one to see what it holds, and nest folders inside folders to keep a long collection in order.
drawer.items_body: Each card here is something you saved, and its icon tells you what: a character, a board, a note, or a card. The card carries a small preview and the date you last touched it, so a familiar item is easy to find. The button up top switches between these rich cards and a tighter list, whichever you prefer.
drawer.seeWorkspace_body: You can drag an item out of the Drawer and onto the sheet or board you are working in. It drops in as a live copy you can edit freely, while the saved original stays untouched in the Drawer.
drawer.search_body: Type here to search your whole library by name at once, across every folder. The Filters button beside it narrows the results further, by kind of item, by game, or by when you last changed it.
drawer.importExport_body: Export sends an item to a file you can back up or hand to another player, and Import brings one of those files back in. It is how you move a character or a board between devices, or share one with your table.
drawer.undoRedo_body: If a change in the Drawer goes wrong, a move, a rename, or a delete, these arrows step it back and forward. They cover the Drawer itself, so a tidy-up gone sideways is never final.
drawer.expand_body: The side panel is handy for a quick reach, but a large library is easier to browse with room to spread out. This button expands the Drawer to fill the width of the screen. Click it to open it up.
drawer.expanded_body: This is the same Drawer with the whole width to lay its items out. It is the place to sort, rename, and tidy in bulk. To drop an item onto the surface behind it, start dragging and hold at the bottom edge, and the Drawer slides aside to let the item through.
drawer.wrap_body: You have opened the Drawer, browsed its folders and items, searched them, and expanded it to full width. It waits behind the button on the side wherever you are, holding everything you save. You can replay this lesson any time from the Learn section in your Settings.

themes.welcome_body: The whole look of the app is a theme, a set of colors you can change or build yourself. This lesson opens your appearance settings, tries a ready-made theme, and points you to where you can craft your own.
themes.mode_body: This switch sets the overall mode. Light and Dark pin it one way, and System follows whatever your device is set to. It is the coarse choice you make before you pick the colors themselves.
themes.themeList_body: Below the mode sit your themes: the presets that ship with the app, and any you have made yourself. Selecting one applies it at once, and the whole app takes on its colors, this window included.
themes.applyPreset_body: Click a theme in the list to put it on, and watch the colors change around you as the app re-skins live. Pick whichever one you like, and the tour moves on.
themes.makeYourOwn_body: This button opens the theme editor. Inside, you set each color by hand for both light and dark, with a live preview beside you as you work. A seed generator can grow a whole palette from a single color when you want a fast start. Presets cannot be edited in place, so the editor hands you an editable copy to build from.
themes.importExport_body: A theme you make can travel. This button imports one from a file, and each of your own themes can be exported the same way from its menu. It is how you hand a palette to another player or carry one between your devices.
themes.wrap_body: You have set the mode, tried a theme, and seen where to build your own. We put your theme back the way you found it, so nothing here changed your setup. You can replay this lesson any time from the Learn section in your Settings.

commandPalette.welcome_body: The command palette is a search bar for actions. It reaches almost everything in the app from the keyboard, so you can create, jump, and run without hunting through menus. This lesson opens it, searches it, runs a command, and walks one of the commands that asks a follow-up.
commandPalette.open_body: The palette answers to a single shortcut from anywhere in the app. Press Ctrl or Cmd + K to summon it now, and it drops in ready for a command.
commandPalette.anatomy_body: At the top is the input where you type, and below it the commands, grouped by what they do. The list is scoped to where you are, so a character sheet offers its cards and trackers while a note or a board offers theirs. Look it over, then continue.
commandPalette.search_body: Type a word and the list narrows to the commands that match. It reads hidden aliases too, so 'die' finds the dice tray and 'roll' finds Roll even though neither word is in the name. Try a query, then continue.
commandPalette.run_body: The highlighted command is the one that runs. Press Enter to run it, or click it with the mouse. Open, find, run is the whole loop.
commandPalette.multiStep_body: Some commands need a detail before they can finish, so they open a follow-up page instead of running at once. Try New Tracker. It asks what kind, then asks you to name it, and creating it lands on the sheet in front of you. Backspace steps back a page if you change your mind.
commandPalette.wrap_body: You have opened the palette, searched it, run a command, and walked one that asked for more. It is always a Ctrl or Cmd + K away, wherever you are. That is the whole desktop app, and you have now seen every part of it. You can replay any lesson from the Learn section in your Settings.

Essence.description: Essence reflects your balance of themes

MobileCharacterSheet.loadCharacterPrompt: Load a character from the drawer or create a new one to get started.
MobileCharacterSheet.drawerHint: Tap the Drawer tab below to browse your saved characters.

welcome.title: Welcome to Campaigns of the Mist
welcome.description: Let's set up your preferences to get you started. You can always get back to this screen from the Settings

appearance.title: Customize Appearance
appearance.description: Choose your theme and color palette

interface.title: Interface Preferences
interface.description: Optimize the interface for your comfort
interface.handednessHint: Controls will be positioned for easier thumb access
interface.navigationHint: Choose how you interact with the app

tips.title: Gesture tips
tips.description: Show one-time hints that introduce each touch gesture the first time you can use it. You can change this anytime in Settings.
tips.reminder: Missed them? You can show the tips again anytime from Settings.

ready.title: You're All Set!
ready.tutorialReminder: You can restart the tutorial anytime from Settings

interface.title: Find your way around
interface.description: A quick look at the desktop workspace

tabs.title: Tabs
tabs.description: Open characters, boards and notes side by side and switch between them from the tab strip.

drawer.title: The Drawer
drawer.description: Organize characters and components in a full file system, then drag them onto your sheet.

board.title: Boards
board.description: Arrange characters, notes and freeform sketches together on an infinite canvas.

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Write rich markdown notes with live preview, links and mentions to tie your world together.

commandPalette.title: Command Palette
commandPalette.description: Press Ctrl+K to run any command straight from the keyboard.

MobileGestureHints.trackerLongPress: Tip: press and hold a tracker to select it for the toolbelt.
MobileGestureHints.drawerLongPress: Tip: press and hold an item to drag it, or tap its ⋯ button for more options.
